title: Define least-privilege agent types via Claude Code --agents config
source: Claude Code subagents / --agents JSON (allowedTools, disallowedTools per agent) (knowledge cutoff Jan 2026; live web search unavailable this run)
description: Specialized Morty roles (reviewer, planner, implementer) appear to all run with the same broad tool access. Claude Code lets you declare named agent types with scoped allowedTools/disallowedTools and a custom system prompt. Scoping tools per role enforces least privilege (e.g. a reviewer Morty cannot Write/Bash, a planner cannot edit files), reducing blast radius and accidental cross-ticket edits, and improves output quality by focusing each agent.
proposed_changes: In persona.py, define an AGENT_PROFILES map (role -> {systemPrompt, allowedTools, disallowedTools}). In delegate.py pass these via `--agents '<json>'` (or per-call --allowedTools/--disallowedTools) when spawning a Morty so reviewers get read-only tools (Read/Grep/Glob), implementers get Edit/Write/Bash scoped to reserved files, planners get no file-mutating tools. Wire team.py to select the profile by assigned role. Log the effective tool scope to roro for auditability.
